proportionality.description: DORA applies tiered requirements based on entity type and size

tiers.name: full
tiers.note: Full ICT risk management framework, incident reporting, resilience testing, third-party risk management

tiers.name: simplified
tiers.applies_to: Entities per Art. 16(1) first subparagraph and microenterprises
tiers.note: Simplified ICT risk management framework — reduced requirements

tiers.name: tlpt_designated
tiers.applies_to: Entities designated by competent authority per Art. 26(8)
tiers.note: TLPT designation based on: (a) impact on financial sector, (b) financial stability concerns, (c) ICT risk profile. Not automatic for any entity type.

derogations.description: Audit rights can be delegated to independent third party appointed by the ICT provider

pitfalls.description: Art. 28(3) para 5 requires timely notification to competent authority of planned contractual arrangements for critical/important functions, and when a function becomes critical/important. This is a distinct obligation from the yearly reporting in para 3.
Art. 30(2) has 9 mandatory elements for ALL ICT contracts. Art. 30(3) adds 6 elements for critical/important functions ONLY. Analysis must map each gap to the correct tier, not conflate them.

Art. 28(8) exit strategies have 5 specific sub-requirements: (1) comprehensive, documented, tested, reviewed periodically, (2) identify alternative solutions, (3) develop transition plans for secure data transfer, (4) enable removal from provider, (5) contingency measures for business continuity. Don't summarize as 'add an exit strategy.'

Art. 30(3)(e)(i) requires UNRESTRICTED rights of access, inspection, and audit — including on-site, with right to take copies, not impeded by other contractual arrangements. Art. 30(3)(e)(iii) adds ICT provider cooperation during on-site inspections. Generic 'audit rights' clauses may not satisfy this.

TLPT applies only to entities designated by competent authorities based on Art. 26(8) criteria (impact, financial stability, ICT risk profile). A payment institution may or may not meet the threshold. Assess applicability before flagging TLPT absence as critical.
The Register of Information has a specific ITS template (DORA_ITS_REGISTER, 2024/2956). Reference the template structure, not just 'maintain a register.'
Art. 30(5) delegates to RTS for further specifying subcontracting assessment criteria for critical/important functions. Check DORA_RTS_ICT_SERVICES for the detailed requirements.

ICT concentration risk operates at two levels: entity-level assessment (Art. 29) and sector-level oversight framework (Art. 31-44 Lead Overseer). Don't conflate entity-level concentration risk with systemic sector-level oversight.

cross_regulation.note: DORA is lex specialis for financial entities on ICT risk management. Where DORA covers a topic, NIS2 does not apply. But NIS2 may still apply for non-ICT aspects.
PSD2 Art. 19 outsourcing notification requirements stack with DORA Art. 28 — both apply to payment institutions.
GDPR Art. 28 processor agreements stack with DORA Art. 30 contractual provisions. Incident reporting timelines differ: DORA Art. 19 vs GDPR Art. 33 (72h).

key_structures.title: General principles — deep structure
key_structures.description: 10 paragraphs. Para 3 has 5 sub-paragraphs: (1) maintain register, (2) distinguish critical/non-critical, (3) yearly reporting to authority, (4) make register available on request, (5) timely notification of planned critical arrangements. Para 8 has 5 exit strategy sub-requirements. Para 9-10 delegate to ITS (register template) and RTS (policy content).

key_structures.title: Key contractual provisions — two-tier structure
key_structures.description: Para 2(a-i): 9 mandatory elements for ALL ICT contracts. Para 3(a-f): 6 additional elements for critical/important functions only — (a) full SLA descriptions, (b) notice/reporting obligations, (c) business contingency, (d) TLPT participation, (e)(i-iv) unrestricted audit rights, (f) exit strategies with mandatory transition period. Para 3 derogation for microenterprises on audit rights.

key_structures.title: TLPT — designation-based, not universal
key_structures.description: Para 8 third sub-paragraph: competent authorities DESIGNATE entities based on (a) impact on financial sector, (b) financial stability concerns including systemic character, (c) ICT risk profile and maturity. Para 8 second sub-paragraph: significant credit institutions must use external testers only. Para 4: pooled testing permitted when provider participation would affect other clients.

key_recitals.clarifies: Register of Information — explains why supervisors need full register access and the regulatory rationale
Incident classification thresholds — explains why materiality thresholds and timelines are calibrated via RTS
Lead Overseer notification framework — explains how oversight recommendations interact with competent authority powers
Delegation framework — explains the Commission's power to adopt RTS/ITS and the ESA role

national_implementation.pattern: Search the relevant Law MCP using get_*_implementations with eu_document_id 'regulation:2022/2554', or search_legislation for the regulation's national complement law

known_examples.title: Lag med kompletterande bestammelser till EU:s forordning om digital operativ motstandskraft for finanssektorn
known_examples.note: Complement law: Finansinspektionen must inform Riksbanken of major ICT incidents (1:4). Financial entities pay annual fees for FI supervision (1:3).
Lag om betaltjanster: payment institutions must notify Finansinspektionen of outsourcing arrangements (stacks with DORA Art. 28)

evidence_hint: Call get_evidence_requirements(regulation='DORA') for per-article audit artifacts with retention periods, auditor questions, and maturity levels. Key artifacts: ICT Risk Management Framework (Art. 6), Third-Party ICT Services Register (Art. 28), Provider Exit Strategy (Art. 28), Concentration Risk Assessment (Art. 29), ICT Contract Template with DORA Clauses (Art. 30).

timeline.note: ICT risk management, simplified framework, incident classification, ICT services policy
Standard templates for Register of Information

citation_format: Cite as: DORA Art. X(Y) for main regulation; Commission Delegated Regulation (EU) YYYY/NNNN, Art. X for RTS; Commission Implementing Regulation (EU) YYYY/NNNN, Art. X for ITS. Never cite by tool name.
